κοινωφελής, ές (s. κοινός, ὠφέλεια; Epict. 4, 10, 12; M. Ant. 1, 16, 4; 3, 4, 1; 4, 12, 2; POxy 1409, 19 [III a.d.]; Philo, De Jos. 34; 73, Mos. 2, 9; 28, Spec. Leg. 4, 157; 170 al.) generally useful ζητεῖν τὸ κ. πᾶσιν seek the common good of all 1 Cl 48:6.—DELG 2 ὀφέλλω.

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

  1. Ant. M. Ant. = Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, II a.d.—List 5

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 554
